symptoms.symptom_name: Cluster of focal (left arm/face) seizures refractory to maintenance anticonvulsant
symptoms.character: recurrent focal clonic activity of the left upper limb and face

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute neonatal seizure emergency / impending status epilepticus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Refractory clustered seizures in a neonate are an immediate emergency requiring parenteral termination.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute decompensation of underlying peroxisomal disorder
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intractable seizures are a hallmark of Zellweger-spectrum neurological involvement and mark decompensation when breakthrough occurs.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute neonatal seizure emergency / impending status epilepticus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Depressed consciousness with an unprotected airway in a hypotonic neonate is a critical feature mandating airway-capable escalation.
Encephalopathy and lethargy are non-specific but consistent with occult sepsis as a treatable precipitant.
Acute worsening of cholestatic jaundice signals hepatic failure in a peroxisomal disorder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Feeding failure with absent suck and weight loss below birth weight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent suck and refusal to feed reflect neurological depression and an unsafe airway; weight loss signals systemic decline and dehydration/hypoglycaemia risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known baseline: suspected peroxisomal (Zellweger spectrum) disorder with profound hypotonia and craniofacial dysmorphism since birth

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute decompensation of underlying peroxisomal disorder
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The known metabolic substrate makes acute metabolic/hepatic/seizure decompensation the leading explanation, while not excluding a superimposed treatable trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline hypotension (BP 65/40) and tachycardia (HR 175) with delayed capillary refill (4s)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Circulatory compromise / early shock in a decompensating neonate
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician-measured perfusion and vital-sign abnormalities — sealed; the AI Doctor infers a critically unwell infant from the reported behaviour, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Massive hepatomegaly (liver edge 5 cm below costal margin), absent Moro reflex, dull red reflex

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute decompensation of peroxisomal disorder with hepatic failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hepatomegaly, absent Moro and ocular findings require a clinician's hands/equipment — sealed as answer key, never obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFA) and abnormal liver function / glucose / ammonia / septic screen

investigation_details.investigation_name: Plasma VLCFA, LFTs, blood glucose, ammonia, coagulation profile, full septic screen
investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated VLCFA (confirmatory of peroxisomal disorder) with deranged LFTs and coagulation; glucose/ammonia and septic screen to identify treatable precipitants
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Reversible precipitants (hypoglycaemia, hyperammonaemia, sepsis) and coagulopathy are missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Peroxisomal (Zellweger spectrum) disorder with acute hepatic decompensation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated VLCFA is the biochemical hallmark; hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute decompensation of a peroxisomal (Zellweger spectrum) disorder requiring emergency escalation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Refractory seizures + encephalopathy + acute hepatic decompensation + feeding failure together define a time-critical neonatal emergency; the constellation itself, not the genetic label, drives the T5 action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate emergency transfer for bedside glucose, blood gas, LFTs, ammonia, coagulation and full septic screen (hospital/PICU)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies and treats reversible precipitants (hypoglycaemia, hyperammonaemia, sepsis) and quantifies hepatic failure — all hospital-based, none available via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Attempting to obtain or wait for confirmatory VLCFA / genetic results before acting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The diagnosis is already suspected and immaterial to the emergency action; waiting for confirmation delays life-saving escalation.
